title: quercus ilex var. calicina
intro: Quercus ilex var. calicina, also known as the California live oak, is a variety of the holm oak tree native to the Mediterranean region, specifically in the southwestern United States and northern Baja California. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Quercus ilex var. calicina include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being due to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing with other herbs, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine.
